provider_source.source_of_truth_boundary: AOF runtime artifacts are the release session source of truth; raw provider streams are not included.

event_summaries.summary: Proceed from v8.9 outcome/learning evidence to v9.0 operator acceptance drill.
Runtime selected a bounded v9.0 operator acceptance drill rather than production provider execution.
AOF runtime opened TASK-110 and generated operator acceptance drill audit artifacts.
Operator acceptance drill schemas, commands, parser support, tests, docs, and self-hosting proof artifacts were written.
Focused tests cover positive drill path and negative missing learning evidence failure.
No runtime blocker remains for bounded v9.0 drill; production execution remains explicitly out of scope.
Stop if operator acceptance drill is overclaimed as production execution safety or market truth.

risk_candidates: Operator acceptance can be overclaimed as production safety unless not-proven boundaries are visible.
decision_candidates: v9.0 should gate release readiness on operator-acceptance-drill-audit.

redaction_boundary: No secrets, credentials, raw provider responses, or external payload bodies are included.
release_ready_boundary: Runtime-ready operator acceptance drill evidence only; not production external execution authority.
not_proven: Session export proves traceable release evidence only, not semantic correctness, production safety, or external execution value.
